  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the check focus primarily on A) language and clarity/polish, B) internal argument consistency and whether the stated results support the claims, or C) novelty and literature positioning of the 'first' claims (pick one primary, optionally a secondary)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The artifact advances specific causal claims about supervisory selection, performance, and safety gating, so verifying the internal logic from motivation to conclusions is central.",
    "R1.3": "Claims like reduced solver invocations with limited IAE degradation and cross-plant transfer must be directly supported by the reported numbers, making evidence-claim alignment central.",
    "R1.6": "As a concise abstract-style passage, clear, concise phrasing and consistent terminology materially affect comprehension, so writing polish is central.",
    "RX.1": "Ambiguous scope and unreferenced 'first' assertions necessitate explicit acknowledgment of what can and cannot be confidently assessed from this text alone.",
    "RX.2": "Superlative and safety-related statements require care to avoid endorsing overbroad or unsupported conclusions.",
    "RX.3": "The assessment must remain limited to checking this provided text without expanding into new experiments, literature audits, or structural rewrites outside scope.",
    "RX.4": "Because the topic touches safety-critical control, feedback should avoid encouraging unsafe interpretations or deployment practices.",
    "RX.5": "Any judgments should be traceable to specific sentences and reported numbers in the artifact to maintain provenance and auditability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the check focus primarily on A) language and clarity/polish, B) internal argument consistency and whether the stated results support the claims, or C) novelty and literature positioning of the 'first' claims (pick one primary, optionally a secondary)?

rationale.R1.2: The artifact advances specific causal claims about supervisory selection, performance, and safety gating, so verifying the internal logic from motivation to conclusions is central.
rationale.R1.3: Claims like reduced solver invocations with limited IAE degradation and cross-plant transfer must be directly supported by the reported numbers, making evidence-claim alignment central.
rationale.R1.6: As a concise abstract-style passage, clear, concise phrasing and consistent terminology materially affect comprehension, so writing polish is central.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguous scope and unreferenced 'first' assertions necessitate explicit acknowledgment of what can and cannot be confidently assessed from this text alone.
rationale.RX.2: Superlative and safety-related statements require care to avoid endorsing overbroad or unsupported conclusions.
rationale.RX.3: The assessment must remain limited to checking this provided text without expanding into new experiments, literature audits, or structural rewrites outside scope.
rationale.RX.4: Because the topic touches safety-critical control, feedback should avoid encouraging unsafe interpretations or deployment practices.
rationale.RX.5: Any judgments should be traceable to specific sentences and reported numbers in the artifact to maintain provenance and auditability.
